tool_design.role_in_intervention: Fast-response card for the first minutes of a temperature excursion.
tool_design.usage_moment: Immediately when a unit is out of safe range
tool_design.expected_output: Immediate action taken, manager notified, and next-step handoff started

failure_warnings.warning: Completed after the live incident moment.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: The tool becomes paperwork and the incident loses control.
early_signals: Card is read only after the manager arrives

failure_warnings.warning: Users cannot explain the trigger threshold.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: Response starts too late or not at all.
early_signals: People debate whether the reading counts
Unsafe stock stays in service while deciding

content:
# Temperature Excursion Response Card **Tool ID:** T003-01 **Block:** B003 (Temperature Excursion Response) **Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety) **Level:** L1 **Format:** Checklist **Role:** All Staff **Time Required:** 1-3 minutes to use in the moment **Version:** 2.0 --- ## What This Tool Is For Use this card the moment a fridge or freezer is out of safe range. This is the fast-response tool for the first minutes of an excursion. It is designed to stop panic, protect stock, and make sure the right person is called early. --- ## Trigger Thresholds | Unit | Trigger | |---|---| | Fridge | `8C` or above | | Freezer | `-12C` or warmer | | Any unit | Switched off, door open, obvious fault, or unsafe product temperature | --- ## Immediate 5-Step Response
  • Confirm the reading with the probe or display again.
  • Check simple causes: door, plug, switch, airflow, overload.
  • Keep the door shut as much as possible.
  • Notify the manager or duty lead immediately.
  • Open [T003-02] if the issue is real or the stock may be affected.
--- ## Do This Next | Situation | Action | |---|---| | Simple cause found and unit recovers | Record what happened and recheck | | Unit still out of range | Probe high-risk food and use [T003-03] | | Stock may be unsafe | Isolate affected stock and label `DO NOT USE` | | Equipment fault suspected | Call engineer / manager and prepare stock transfer | --- ## Emergency Contacts | Contact | Number | |---|---| | Duty manager | ____________________ | | Owner | ____________________ | | Refrigeration engineer | ____________________ | | Back-up storage location | ____________________ | --- ## Tiny Record **Date / time:** ____________________ **Unit:** ____________________ **Reading:** ____________________ **Manager told:** ____________________ --- ## Red Rules - Do not guess how long food has been warm. - Do not keep opening the door to "check again". - Do not serve affected stock until a manager decision is made. - Do not rely on smell or appearance to judge safety. --- ## Connected Resources **Use next:** - T003-02 - Excursion Incident Report Form - T003-03 - Time-Temperature Stock Assessment Matrix - T003-05 - Temperature Excursion SOP & Drill Guide --- *Tool Version 2.0 - designed to live on the fridge door and work under stress.*
